section_name: Reasonable Probability Not Admissible in Civil Suit
text: A determination under Section 601.154 (Department Determination of Probability of Liability) or 601.157 (Hearing Procedures) that there is a reasonable probability that a judgment will be rendered against a person as a result of a collision may not be introduced in evidence in a suit for damages arising from that collision.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 601. Motor Vehicle Safety Responsibility Act
##### § 601.169 — Reasonable Probability Not Admissible in Civil Suit

A determination under Section 601.154 (Department Determination of Probability of Liability) or 601.157 (Hearing Procedures) that there is a reasonable probability that a judgment will be rendered against a person as a result of a collision may not be introduced in evidence in a suit for damages arising from that collision.

*Acts 2023, 88th Leg., R.S., Ch. 709 (H.B.2190), Sec. 85, eff. September 1, 2023.*
